policy_statement: Conserve and rehabilitate watercourses to maintain environmental flows and river continuity in endemic species distribution areas; regulate irrigation abstractions from catchments serving as summer refuges; install/adapt fish transposition devices on weirs.
mechanism_description: It conserves and rehabilitates watercourses, regulates irrigation abstraction volumes in refuge catchments, and installs or adapts fish transposition devices on weirs to maintain environmental flows and river continuity for vulnerable freshwater and migratory fish.
primary_causal_pathway: Climate hazard (warming + drought/altered flows) → ecosystem stress/exposure → improved environmental flows + connectivity → ↓ ecological vulnerability/impact
causal_mechanism_detail:
(1) Change: Manage and rehabilitate river/watercourse systems by conserving/restoring channels and riparian condition, limiting/adjusting irrigation abstractions in refuge catchments, and installing/adapting fish passage structures at barriers (e.g., weirs).  
(2) Mediating process: Maintains environmental flows, improves longitudinal connectivity, and sustains suitable habitat conditions (temperature, dissolved oxygen, refugia availability) that allow migration, spawning, and recolonization, especially during low-flow/high-temperature periods.  
(3) Outcome: Increased resilience of freshwater ecosystems and vulnerable/migratory fish populations to climate variability and change (reduced mortality, fragmentation impacts, and loss of refugia under drought/heat).
dominant_pathway_test: The single biggest climate-relevant effect is reduced vulnerability of freshwater ecosystems and migratory fish to climate-driven low flows and warming through maintained environmental flows and connectivity.
mechanism_classification_reasoning:
1) Dominant causal pathway: addresses climate hazards (drought, reduced summer flows, warming waters) by altering water management and river infrastructure to reduce ecosystem exposure/sensitivity and improve adaptive capacity.  
2) Why Adaptation over alternatives: while river restoration can have minor carbon co-benefits (e.g., riparian biomass) and water-efficiency elements (reduced abstraction), the principal intended and largest effect is safeguarding ecological function and species persistence under climate stress.  
3) Edge cases applied: if implemented primarily for carbon sequestration (e.g., extensive riparian afforestation for sink creation) it could shift toward Nature-Based Solutions; if focused mainly on reducing water demand regardless of ecosystem outcomes it could lean Resource Efficiency. Here, fish passage + environmental flows clearly target resilience/impact reduction.  
4) Applicability to ALL instances of this mechanism: any policy that conserves/rehabilitates watercourses, manages abstractions for refugia, and provides fish passage is fundamentally about maintaining freshwater ecosystem function under changing hydrology—an adaptation objective.  
5) Note on secondary subtypes: any mitigation/NBS/resource-efficiency co-benefits (carbon uptake, water savings, energy impacts) require policy-specific evidence and are deferred to policy-level subclassification.
mechanism_edge_case_notes: Potential co-benefits include riparian carbon sequestration (could be framed as Nature-Based Solutions) and reduced pumping energy from lower abstractions (mitigation), but these are typically secondary to the core ecosystem-resilience objective of environmental flows and connectivity.
key_indicators: “Conservation and rehabilitation of watercourses”; “maintain environmental flows”; “river continuity”; “Regulating the volumes of water abstracted for irrigation”; “Installation or adaptation of transposition devices on weirs”
co_benefits: Biodiversity conservation; improved river water quality and habitat condition; reduced conflict over water use; potential recreational/fisheries benefits
instance_edge_case_notes: Bundled instrument: includes both infrastructure/restoration (fish passage, rehabilitation) and a regulatory element (limits on irrigation abstractions). If split into separate entries, “regulating volumes abstracted” would classify as regulatory-mandate/performance-standard, while fish passage/restoration would remain infrastructure-investment.
